articles.title: The Problem with Binding News and Prediction Markets
articles.summary: News outlets are increasingly partnering with prediction markets or launching their own betting platforms, creating potential conflicts between journalistic integrity and profit motives. When reporters have financial incentives tied to market outcomes, it corrupts the very information flow that makes prediction markets valuable in the first place.

articles.title: First look: Stephanie Cutter to advise fast-growing Kalshi
articles.summary: Stephanie Cutter, the Democratic strategist who ran Obama's 2012 campaign, is joining prediction market Kalshi as an advisor. Her political connections could help Kalshi navigate regulatory hurdles and expand into election betting, where the real money flows during campaign seasons.

articles.title: Polymarket is banned in Ontario. Flyers advertising it were handed out outside a Jays game anyway
articles.summary: Someone was distributing Polymarket flyers outside a Toronto Blue Jays game despite the platform being banned in Ontario. The guerrilla marketing shows how prediction markets are finding creative ways to reach users even when regulators try to block access.

articles.title: As hoops betting spikes, it's New Hampshire and other states vs. prediction markets
articles.summary: New Hampshire and other states are cracking down on prediction markets as March Madness betting reaches fever pitch. The regulatory pushback threatens to fragment the prediction market landscape just as mainstream adoption was gaining serious momentum.

articles.title: Prediction markets promised more clarity in elections. The reality is messier.
articles.summary: Prediction markets were supposed to cut through polling noise and give us cleaner election forecasts, but they're delivering their own brand of confusion and volatility. The gap between promise and reality matters because it shapes how seriously traders, media, and politicians take these markets as decision-making tools.

articles.title: What will Trump say during Address to the Nation on April 1?
articles.summary: Trump plans to deliver an Address to the Nation on April 1, creating immediate speculation about whether this represents a genuine policy announcement or an elaborate April Fools' prank. Bettors are already weighing odds on everything from immigration policy rollouts to trademark Trump theatrics, with the timing adding a wild card element that could make even seasoned political predictors second-guess their positions.

articles.title: California's top lawyer explains why he hasn't sued Kalshi like Nevada and Arizona
articles.summary: California's attorney general is defending his decision not to follow Nevada and Arizona in filing lawsuits against prediction market Kalshi. The Golden State's hands-off approach could signal broader regulatory acceptance for political betting markets, potentially opening the floodgates for other states to embrace prediction platforms.

articles.title: Trump's Speech Skipped 'Ceasefire' And 'Peace', Leaving Polymarket Traders Who Bet Against These Words Pr
articles.summary: Trump delivered a speech that notably avoided mentioning "ceasefire" or "peace," words that Polymarket traders had bet against appearing in his remarks. The absence of these terms likely paid out handsomely for contrarian bettors who correctly predicted Trump would steer clear of dovish language, demonstrating how word-specific markets can reward close attention to a politician's rhetorical patterns.

articles.title: Opinion: Prediction Markets Make a Bet Against Public Health
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets are fundamentally at odds with public health outcomes, probably claiming they incentivize betting against positive health developments. This matters because it hits the core tension in prediction markets: whether accurate price discovery conflicts with socially beneficial outcomes, which could fuel regulatory backlash if the argument gains traction.

articles.title: Polymarket and other prediction platforms driving oil market, traders say
articles.summary: Oil traders are increasingly using Polymarket and similar prediction platforms to inform their commodity trading strategies. This marks a major shift where betting markets are now influencing traditional financial markets instead of just reflecting them.

articles.title: ‘Death Markets’ and Attack Ads: Prediction Rivalry Turns Nasty
articles.summary: Prediction market platforms are apparently slinging mud at each other in attack ads, with rivals throwing around inflammatory terms like "death markets" to describe assassination betting pools. The nastiness signals the industry is growing big enough for serious competitive warfare, which could either legitimize prediction markets through mainstream attention or torpedo them if regulators start paying attention to the drama.

articles.title: JPMorgan Mulls Prediction Markets Business
articles.summary: JPMorgan is reportedly exploring whether to launch its own prediction markets platform, joining the growing list of major financial institutions eyeing the space. If the banking giant moves forward, it could bring massive institutional capital and mainstream legitimacy to prediction markets, potentially dwarfing current platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket.
